title: Q-learning vs Monte Carlo — 100k-episode win-rate plot, ε=0.005 final choice
html:
Yahir's RL writeup section — "Experiment Setup and Results." Ran Q learning for 100k episodes with ε-greedy at fixed ε=0.005 on his MacBook M1 locally. Started with higher ε, found exploit-heavy beat explore-heavy → settled on 0.005. Plot compares Q-learning vs Monte Carlo vs a 50% baseline; win-rate hovering ~0.46-0.48 with Q-learning edging Monte Carlo.

TL;DR

  • Empirical lesson: for this task, lower ε (0.005) outperformed higher ε after 100k episodes — the exploration ceiling was lower than expected.
  • All training run locally on Yahir's M1 MacBook — no GPU, no cluster. 100k episodes feasible on consumer Apple Silicon.
  • Results: Q learning ≈ 0.48 win-rate, slightly above Monte Carlo, both above the 50% baseline (well, ~0.46-0.48 — below 0.5; the task may have negative-EV under random play).

q learning · reinforcement learning · m1 mac · osu · sarsa
